paper_title: Field data acquisition and analysis of Antarctic ice sheets

methods.method_name: Airborne Radio Echo Sounding System Development
methods.description: Development and modification of the INGV-IT airborne radio echo sounding system for mapping ice thickness and internal layers of glaciers, ice sheets, and ice shelves in Antarctica.

steps.description: The RES system has been slightly modified during each expedition.
steps.searchable_summary: modify RES system for each expedition

steps.description: Main radar system improvements have been applied between 1996 and 2001 concerning the total range time, the number of samples for each radar track, and the number of acquired tracks per second.

methods.description: Processing and analysis of RES data to determine ice thickness and internal layering, including deconvolution, gain adjustment, filtering, data stacking, and migration.

steps.description: RES raw data post-processing flow consisted basically in deconvolution, gain adjustment, vertical and horizontal filtering, data stacking and, in some cases, migration.
steps.searchable_summary: process RES raw data with deconvolution, gain adjustment, filtering, stacking, and migration

steps.description: All data were processed following the same criteria and finally were cross-checked.
Crossover point analysis pointed out that ice thickness values in different RES profiles are in good agreement.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze crossover points for ice thickness agreement

steps.description: Ice thickness was calculated using a constant velocity of 168 m µs −1.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ice thickness using constant velocity

steps.description: Whole 2001 and 2003 RES data sets have been used to model the three-dimensional view of the bedrock topography and of the internal layering.
steps.searchable_summary: model 3D view of bedrock topography and internal layering
outputs: 3D model of bedrock topography and internal layering

steps.description: All thickness profiles data sets have been gridded by Kriging method with a square grid cell size of about 500 m.
steps.searchable_summary: grid thickness profiles using Kriging method
